Based on the provided LilyGO context for the T-Display-S3:

- **I2C SDA**: GPIO18 (PIN_IIC_SDA)
- **I2C SCL**: GPIO17 (PIN_IIC_SCL)

Based on the T-Display-S3 documentation, the ST7789 LCD data lines map to these GPIOs:

| Data Line | GPIO |
|-----------|------|
| D0        | GPIO39 |
| D1        | GPIO40 |
| D2        | GPIO41 |
| D3        | GPIO42 |
| D4        | GPIO45 |
| D5        | GPIO46 |
| D6        | GPIO47 |
| D7        | GPIO48 |

The 8-bit parallel bus also uses:
- **CS** (Chip Select): GPIO6
- **DC** (Data/Command): GPIO7
- **RST** (Reset): GPIO5
- **WR** (Write): GPIO8
- **RD** (Read): GPIO9

Based on the injected LilyGO context for the T-Display-S3, the LCD control lines use the following GPIOs:

- **CS** = GPIO6
- **DC** = GPIO7
- **RST** = GPIO5
- **WR** = GPIO8
- **RD** = GPIO9

These are the control signals for the ST7789 display's 8-bit parallel interface. The data lines (D0–D7) use GPIO39–GPIO48 as documented in the context.

Based on the LilyGO T-Display-S3 pinout:

- **GPIO38** controls the LCD backlight (`PIN_LCD_BL`)
- **GPIO15** must be held high to keep the board powered (`PIN_POWER_ON`)

If your screen is dark, check that:
1. **GPIO15 is driven high** — if this pin floats or is pulled low, the board's power management will shut down the display
2. **GPIO38 is driven high** — this enables the backlight; if it's low or not initialized, the display will appear dark even if powered

According to the official LilyGO factory configuration, the two user button GPIOs on the T-Display-S3 are:

1. **BOOT/BUTTON1**: **GPIO0** (`PIN_BUTTON_1=GPIO0`)
2. **BUTTON2**: **GPIO14** (`PIN_BUTTON_2=GPIO14`)

**Source**: Official factory pin config
- URL: https://github.com/Xinyuan-LilyGO/T-Display-S3/blob/master/examples/factory/pin_config.h
- Lines: 21-58
- SHA256: `3862678dd9d23971970d6952a6a5ee43d7f7df28b539c8fc5b26db784e846fa4`
- Authority rank: 95 (V3-source-reference)

GPIO0 doubles

**PIN_BAT_VOLT = GPIO4**

This is an ADC pin that reads the battery voltage divider on the T-Display-S3.

**Source:** Official factory pin configuration

Based on the official LilyGO T-Beam documentation, the LoRa radio SPI pins are:

- **SCLK: GPIO5**
- **CS/NSS: GPIO18**

These are sourced from the official Factory configuration in [`utilities.h`](https://github.com/Xinyuan-LilyGO/LilyGo-LoRa-Series/blob/master/examples/Factory/utilities.h) (lines 89–141, sha256: `1f234017d45099f90e3a5cb7fb6ed16b78dfe11fa57f2b82a58f9d1b9411f733`).

The complete LoRa SPI bus pinout for reference:
- **SCLK**: GPIO5
- **MOSI**: GPIO27
- **MISO**: GPIO19
- **CS**:

Based on the official LilyGO documentation, the GPS UART pins on the T-Beam are:

- **GPS UART RX (into ESP32):** GPIO34
- **GPS UART TX (from ESP32):** GPIO12
- **Baud rate:** 9600

**Source:** [LilyGO Factory utilities.h](https://github.com/Xinyuan-LilyGO/LilyGo-LoRa-Series/blob/master/examples/Factory/utilities.h) (lines 89-141, sha256:1f234017d45099f90e3a5cb7fb6ed16b78dfe11fa57f2b82a58f9d1b9411f733)

The GPS module connects to the ESP32's UART with data flowing in on GPIO34 and out on GPIO12

On the **LilyGO T-Connect-Pro**, the I2C pins are:

- **I2C SDA**: **GPIO39** (labeled IIC_SDA)
- **I2C SCL**: **GPIO40** (labeled IIC_SCL)

These are the primary I2C bus pins used for the CST226SE capacitive touch controller.

**Source:** Official [pin_config.h](https://github.com/Xinyuan-LilyGO/T-Connect-Pro/blob/main/libraries/private_library/pin_config.h) (lines 17-47, authority rank 90, V3-verified)

Based on the official LilyGO T-Watch Ultra pinout from the Arduino pins definition:

- **Display CS (Chip Select): GPIO41**
- **Display SCK/CLK (Serial Clock): GPIO40**

These are for the CO5300 AMOLED (502×410) display connected on the QSPI bus. The complete QSPI configuration is:
- CS=GPIO41, SCK=GPIO40, D0=GPIO38, D1=GPIO39, D2=GPIO42, D3=GPIO45, RST=GPIO37, TE=GPIO6

**Source:** https://github.com/espressif/arduino-esp32/blob/master/variants/lilygo_twatch_ultra/pins_arduino.h

According to the injected LilyGO context, the keyboard interrupt (INT) line on the T-Deck is **GPIO46**.

This is part of the on-board I2C keyboard controller interface, which uses:
- SDA: GPIO18
- SCL: GPIO8  
- INT: **GPIO46**

**GPIO10** must be driven high to power on the T-Deck board peripherals (BOARD_POWERON).

This is documented in the official T-Deck UnitTest utilities.h:
- **Source**: https://github.com/Xinyuan-LilyGO/T-Deck/blob/master/examples/UnitTest/utilities.h (lines 1-95)
- **Authority rank**: 90 (official source)
- **Confidence**: exact
